skill.name: robotics-subject-expert
skill.description: Domain guidance for Physical AI, ROS 2, robot simulation, and humanoid robotics concepts.
skill.summary: Explains robotics concepts and ROS 2 workflows for learning, planning, and troubleshooting.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported three external command findings and two blocker-style findings, but all are false positives in this Markdown-only skill. Lines 24-26 contain inline code paths, and line 3 is a plain subject description with no weak cryptography, reconnaissance, executable code, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Interpreted as Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but lines 24-26 only use Markdown inline code to name module, lab, and capstone paths. No executable command, shell expansion, script, or user input execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown bullets and names documentation paths only. There is no Ruby file, shell script, command invocation, or executable context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at line 3, but the line only describes Physical AI, ROS 2, and Humanoid Robotics. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter description text and contains no cryptographic code or algorithm reference. The finding appears to be a keyword collision.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at line 3, but the line is a subject-area description. It does not request system inspection, environment enumeration, filesystem access, or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only evidence is a plain metadata description. No commands, APIs, or instructions for reconnaissance are present in the reviewed file.

content.user_title: Explain Robotics Concepts With ROS 2 Context
content.value_statement: Robotics learners often need clear links between AI concepts, ROS 2 workflows, and simulation tools. This skill provides focused explanations, project guidance, and troubleshooting support for Physical AI topics.
actual_capabilities: Explains Physical AI concepts such as embodied intelligence, SLAM, inverse kinematics, and reinforcement learning.
Provides ROS 2 learning guidance with emphasis on Humble Hawksbill concepts and workflows.
Helps structure robotics modules, labs, and capstone project plans.
Offers conceptual troubleshooting support for Isaac Sim, Gazebo, and simulation workflows.
Can produce beginner-friendly ROS 2 Python or C++ examples when asked.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, tools, or automated robotics setup commands.
Does not verify robot hardware safety, sensor calibration, or real-world deployment readiness.
May need project files, logs, or launch details for precise troubleshooting.
Covers broad subject guidance, not a complete robotics textbook.

use_cases.title: Learn ROS 2 Fundamentals
use_cases.description: Get clear explanations of ROS 2 nodes, topics, services, and package structure before building labs.

use_cases.title: Plan Simulation Exercises
use_cases.description: Design Gazebo or Isaac Sim lab activities that connect perception, control, and robot behavior.

use_cases.title: Scope a Capstone Project
use_cases.description: Break a robotics idea into modules for perception, planning, control, simulation, and evaluation.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Concept
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain inverse kinematics for a beginner robotics student. Use a simple robot arm example and define each key term.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Simulation Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Gazebo and Isaac Sim for a ROS 2 Humble lab. Focus on learning value, setup complexity, and realistic simulation.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Lab
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a one-week ROS 2 lab about SLAM. Include learning goals, setup assumptions, tasks, expected outputs, and reflection questions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Capstone Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a capstone architecture for a humanoid robot that uses vision, navigation, and reinforcement learning. Include modules, interfaces, risks, and evaluation metrics.

output_examples.input: Explain SLAM for a ROS 2 beginner.
output_examples.output: A concise explanation that separates mapping, localization, sensor input, robot motion, and common ROS 2 packages used in learning projects.

output_examples.input: Help plan a Gazebo lab for robot navigation.
Review a humanoid robotics capstone idea.
output_examples.output: A structured project outline covering perception, control, simulation, evaluation, and practical scope limits.

best_practices: Provide the ROS 2 version, simulator, robot model, and goal before asking for troubleshooting help.
Ask for diagrams, module breakdowns, or learning objectives when planning labs or capstones.
Separate simulation assumptions from real robot assumptions in every design discussion.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for real hardware deployment advice without safety constraints and hardware details.
Do not treat generated examples as validated control software for physical robots.
Do not mix Isaac Sim, Gazebo, and ROS 2 assumptions without stating the intended environment.

faq.question: What robotics topics does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Physical AI, ROS 2, simulation, inverse kinematics, SLAM, reinforcement learning, computer vision, and humanoid robotics concepts.

faq.question: Can it write ROS 2 examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It can provide educational Python or C++ examples when you describe the ROS 2 concept or workflow you need.

faq.question: Does it install ROS 2 or simulators?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains guidance only and does not run installers, commands, or setup scripts.

faq.question: Is this safe for real robot control?
faq.answer: Use it for learning and planning only. Real robot control requires hardware review, testing, and safety validation.

faq.question: Which ROS 2 version is referenced?
faq.answer: The source material references ROS 2 Humble Hawksbill as the main learning version.

faq.question: Can it help with capstone planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can break a robotics capstone into modules, interfaces, milestones, risks, and evaluation methods.
